key_points: Dorms appear functional but limited firsthand detail available
Campus beauty frequently praised; living environment benefits from it
Small enrollment means housing community is tight-knit
key_quotes: I couldn't see the dorms again because of corona and i might be going next year
Top 10 most beautiful campuses, you're so lucky

key_points: Greek life dominates campus social scene uncomfortably for non-members
Described as a 'bubble' — hard to escape campus culture
Preppy, white, straight-leaning vibe reported; LGBTQ+ POC students warned
key_quotes: The guys gave me date rapey lax bro vibes
The girls were blond and sporty lookin
It's a small uni and a lot of people say it's a bubble, but that's a problem cuz u can't get anywhere without a car

key_points: Dining hall repeatedly praised as genuinely excellent for a small school
Weekly menus reportedly include high-quality food like flank steak
One of Richmond's most consistently praised features across sources
key_quotes: Excellent food (look at their weekly menus, like wtf they serve flank steak and...)
I could never go there because I literally live within walking distance (but I do love their dining hall)

key_points: Social environment described as 'incredibly toxic' by at least one student
Bubble campus with limited escape options may intensify pressure
No specific mental health resources mentioned positively or negatively
key_quotes: Their academics, programs, etc are really good but I think their social environment is incredibly toxic
It's a small uni and a lot of people say it's a bubble

key_points: Full ride Richmond Scholars program is genuinely transformative and generous
Virginia residents under $75k income get a guaranteed full ride
Financial aid can be cut dramatically after freshman year if family income rises
key_quotes: Absolutely everything was covered by my financial aid the first two years, but the package I received for the upcoming year left a $20,000 gap
International: waitlist — 78k contribution or you are out
University of Richmond rejected me — they said it's aid related

key_points: Student-to-teacher ratio reportedly as low as 5:1; professors accessible
Slight grade deflation noted alongside W&M and W&L as Virginia norm
CS department seen as weak enough that one student considered transferring to VT
key_quotes: The student-to-teacher ratio is like 5:1, so I can easily get research opportunities
W&L, W&M and UoR, as virginia colleges, all have slight grade deflation
The computer science department is stronger at tech which will open up more internships for me

key_points: Admissions-financial aid miscommunication resulted in a scholarship offer to a rejected student
Financial aid office can gut packages mid-enrollment with little warning
Waitlist process uses phone calls, not email — unusual and stressful
key_quotes: Admissions and Financial aid miscommunicated with our department as we made our decisions
Received a text saying: The University of Richmond would like to update you on your waitlist status. If you are still interested in Richmond, call [number]
I found out University of Richmond doesn't email you for notification of your acceptance/denial, you have to wait for a letter

key_points: Campus consistently called one of the most beautiful in the US
Richmond city (RVA) is vibrant but requires a car to access
Campus bubble is real — students warn not to get 'stuck' on campus
key_quotes: Make sure you get off campus and don't get stuck in the 'Richmond bubble' — RVA is awesome
imo one of the prettiest campuses in all of us colleges
It's a small uni and a lot of people say it's a bubble, but that's a problem cuz u can't get anywhere without a car

key_points: Big 4 accounting firms actively recruit from Robinson School of Business
CS students feel VT offers stronger internship pipelines than Richmond
Strong alumni network mentioned but limited concrete outcome data shared
key_quotes: My dad works for a big 4 firm and they actively recruit from UR
The computer science department is stronger at tech which will open up more internships for me
My nephew just graduated last year and absolutely LOVED it! He now has an amazing job that he loves

key_points: Not worth full tuition — multiple commenters explicitly state this
Exceptional value with merit aid or Virginia Promise; terrible value at sticker price
Mid-enrollment financial aid cuts can make a 'affordable' school suddenly unaffordable
key_quotes: Good education. A little snobby. NOT worth paying full-tuition to attend though.
Absolutely everything was covered by my financial aid the first two years, but the package I received for the upcoming year left a $20,000 gap
Richmond is a rich kids school with a high percentage of recruited athletes that are happy to pay full price

key_points: Wealthy, preppy, Greek-dominated culture that can feel exclusionary
Beautiful campus softens the experience but doesn't fix social issues
Best fit for students with strong financial aid who want LAC academics
key_quotes: Some people say the culture is white, straight, and pretentious and others say it's laid back, calm, and welcoming
Richmond is a rich kids school with a high percentage of recruited athletes
Please look at the Instagram accounts @abolishrichmondgreeklife and @protectourweb to get an idea. The way UR has handled recent issues regarding racism is disgusting

key_points: Racism: student graduating in 2022 called admin response to racism 'disgusting'
Greek Life: Greek system criticized loudly enough to spawn abolition Instagram account
Financial Aid: packages can collapse mid-enrollment leaving students $20k+ in debt
key_quotes: Please look at the Instagram accounts @abolishrichmondgreeklife and @protectourweb. The way UR has handled recent issues regarding racism is disgusting
Absolutely everything was covered by my financial aid the first two years, but the package I received for the upcoming year left a $20,000 gap, even after 7k in loans
If anyone in the comments doesn't know, she ended up transferring out. If you're thinking about attending, I recommend watching that video too

key_points: Richmond Scholars full ride ($340k+) is one of the best LAC merit awards nationally
Virginia residents under $75k income get a guaranteed full ride — underadvertised
Robinson School of Business has genuine Big 4 recruiting pipelines most don't know about
key_quotes: I was selected as a Richmond Scholar which is a full ride to Urichmond worth $340,000+
If your family income is less than $75,000 a year, and you're a Virginia resident, Richmond guarantees a full ride
My dad works for a big 4 firm and they actively recruit from UR. The students are bright and a great fit
